title: achillea ceretanica
intro: Achillea ceretanica, also known as the Corsican yarrow, is a perennial plant species in the sunflower family native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can be attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The health benefits of Achillea ceretanica include reducing fever, soothing digestive issues, and relieving menstrual cramps, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Achillea ceretanica can be prepared as an herbal tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
